artPrompt: An ornate hero shot of a frosted service window in a beige wall, a small brass call-bell and a ticket spike on its ledge, a closed shutter half-lowered, cold cyan arcane light glowing through the pebbled glass, a faint shimmer rising from blank permit slips, the corridor beyond dissolving into ivory haze, a quiet bureaucratic loneliness, rendered as soft pastel impressionism — hazy diffuse light, visible chalky texture, gentle gradients, dreamy muted color.
name: The Permit Office, Window 3
text:
Before any opponent rolls a die or flips a coin, they must first take a number and pay {1} for a permit. If they don't pay, the roll or flip doesn't happen and you may say "Window's closed" out loud.
{2}, {T}: You issue yourself a permit. Roll a six-sided die. You may keep that result on file and apply it to your next die roll this game, ignoring whatever you actually roll then.
flavorText: Window 1 and Window 2 have been "temporarily closed" since the previous administration.
